title: Cobalt blue underglaze painting on stoneware
notes: Brushing pre-mixed blue underglaze onto bisqueware, then covering with a transparent glaze for glaze firing. Discusses brush types (liner for fine lines, mop for washes), applying two or three coats for opacity, and leaving areas bare for glaze-only zones. The classic blue-and-white technique. Fire at cone 6. Safety note for raw cobalt oxide (not applicable here as pre-mixed underglaze is used, but worth mentioning for context).
